:::{#thm-vcov-elements}

#### Elements of the variance-covariance matrix are pairwise covariances

For a $p \times 1$ random vector $\vX = (X_1, \ldots, X_p)'$,
the $(i,j)$-th element of $\Varf{\vX}$ is $\Covs{X_i}{X_j}$:

$$
\Varf{\vX}=
\pmat{
\Varf{X_1}      & \Covs{X_1}{X_2} & \cdots & \Covs{X_1}{X_p} \\
\Covs{X_2}{X_1} & \Varf{X_2}      & \cdots & \Covs{X_2}{X_p} \\
\vdots          & \vdots          & \ddots & \vdots           \\
\Covs{X_p}{X_1} & \Covs{X_p}{X_2} & \cdots & \Varf{X_p}
}
$$

:::

---

:::{.proof}

Let $\mu_i = \E{X_i}$ for $i = 1, \ldots, p$,
so $\Expp\vX = (\mu_1, \ldots, \mu_p)'$.
By @def-cov-vec-x:

$$
\ba
\Varf{\vX}
&= \Expf{
  \paren{\vX - \Expp\vX}
  \tp{\paren{\vX - \Expp\vX}}
}
\\
&= \Expf{
  \pmat{X_1 - \mu_1 \\ \vdots \\ X_p - \mu_p}
  \pmat{X_1 - \mu_1 & \cdots & X_p - \mu_p}
}
\\
&= \Expf{
  \pmat{
    (X_1 - \mu_1)(X_1 - \mu_1) & \cdots & (X_1 - \mu_1)(X_p - \mu_p) \\
    \vdots                      & \ddots & \vdots                      \\
    (X_p - \mu_p)(X_1 - \mu_1) & \cdots & (X_p - \mu_p)(X_p - \mu_p)
  }
}
\\
&=
  \pmat{
    \Expf{(X_1 - \mu_1)(X_1 - \mu_1)} & \cdots & \Expf{(X_1 - \mu_1)(X_p - \mu_p)} \\
    \vdots                             & \ddots & \vdots                             \\
    \Expf{(X_p - \mu_p)(X_1 - \mu_1)} & \cdots & \Expf{(X_p - \mu_p)(X_p - \mu_p)}
  }
\\
&=
  \pmat{
    \Covs{X_1}{X_1} & \cdots & \Covs{X_1}{X_p} \\
    \vdots          & \ddots & \vdots           \\
    \Covs{X_p}{X_1} & \cdots & \Covs{X_p}{X_p}
  }
\\
&=
  \pmat{
    \Varf{X_1}      & \cdots & \Covs{X_1}{X_p} \\
    \vdots          & \ddots & \vdots           \\
    \Covs{X_p}{X_1} & \cdots & \Varf{X_p}
  }
\ea
$$

where:

- the step from the third to fourth line uses the fact that
  expectation distributes element-wise over matrices,
- the step from the fourth to fifth line uses @def-cov, and
- the last step uses @lem-cov-xx.

:::
